canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, AfriCOMET is a measurement instrument used to assess translation quality. It is defined as an enhanced version of the COMET metric specifically adapted for African languages. This adaptation is achieved by incorporating an "African-centric encoder, AfroXLM-R," and by training the model on African language data. The stated goal of this instrument is to improve translation evaluation performance for this specific language group. One paper also refers to a specific version of the instrument, "AfriCOMETv1.1-MTL".
papers: Attacking Misinformation Detection Using Adversarial Examples Generated by Language Models
CAFE: Retrieval Head-based Coarse-to-Fine Information Seeking to Enhance Multi-DocumentQACapability
MessIRve: A Large-ScaleSpanish Information Retrieval Dataset

definitions.text: An enhanced version of COMET for African languages that uses an Africa-centric encoder (AfroXLM-R) and is trained on African language data to improve translation evaluation performance.
definitions.paper: CAFE: Retrieval Head-based Coarse-to-Fine Information Seeking to Enhance Multi-DocumentQACapability

relationships.paper: Speculative Diffusion Decoding: Accelerating Language Generation through Diffusion
